title: AMM Launch Readiness: Proof + Test + Security Gate Hardening
scope_summary: Closed theorem and simulation gaps in the AMM proof chain.
Extended differential fuzzing and invariant campaign depth.
Pinned toolchain versions and stabilized release-gate CI artifacts.
baseline_risks: Inconsistent release evidence across formal, test, and static-analysis layers.
High probability of launch delay due to unresolved refinement assumptions.
Weak reviewer confidence from non-reproducible build and security outputs.
interventions: Implemented floor-bounded refinement proofs with explicit assumptions.
Added two-actor invariant harness and mixed-operation stateful fuzzing.
Introduced reproducible reviewer/commercial package generation with checksums.
outcomes: Client moved from ad-hoc validation to repeatable release evidence.
Audit and launch-readiness conversations accelerated with artifact-backed claims.
Post-sprint conversion path opened for retainer ownership of release gates.
anonymization_notes: Protocol and stakeholder names replaced with aliases.
No private code or infrastructure details included.
Metrics rounded where needed to avoid deanonymization.

quote.text: The integrated proof/test/security package gave us launch confidence and shortened review cycles.
